title: Biological homochirality and symmetry-breaking amplification
description: A bounded node separating observed biological handedness, demonstrated asymmetric autocatalysis, and unresolved origin mechanisms.
definitions: Terrestrial proteins predominantly use L-amino acids and nucleic acids predominantly use D-sugars.
Homochirality is strong enrichment of one enantiomeric family.
The Soai reaction demonstrates asymmetric autocatalytic amplification in a laboratory chemical system.
assumptions: The kinetic equations are a simplified symmetry-breaking model, not a complete Soai mechanism.
Parameters, initial conditions, noise and environmental boundaries must be declared.
Parity violation, minerals, photochemistry, extraterrestrial delivery and flow are candidate mechanisms, not a settled cause.
No universal role for an 8 Hz drive in prebiotic homochirality is established.
mechanism: A small deterministic or stochastic chiral imbalance may be amplified when reaction kinetics provide same-handed positive feedback and sufficient cross-inhibition or selection.

evidence.source: Established stereochemistry of terrestrial proteins and nucleic acids
evidence.notes: Supports biological homochirality, not a unique origin mechanism.

evidence.source: Laboratory asymmetric autocatalysis exemplified by the Soai reaction
evidence.notes: Demonstrates amplification in a particular chemical system.
Demonstrates possible amplification under declared assumptions.

predictions: For symmetric initial conditions and epsilon = 0, a deterministic symmetric model remains racemic unless instability or noise breaks symmetry.
Increasing abs(epsilon) should reduce the expected time to a declared ee threshold in an amplifying parameter regime.
Any frequency-specific effect requires sham and neighboring-frequency controls.
falsification_conditions: Independent implementations cannot reproduce the declared trajectories.
A proposed origin mechanism produces no reproducible bias under blinded controls.
A claimed frequency effect does not differ from sham or neighboring-frequency controls.
reference_frame: Declared reaction network, environment, concentrations, temperature and time interval
normalization_method: Non-negative concentrations and enantiomeric excess in [0,1]
normalization_claim: ee is bounded to [0,1] when x+y is non-zero.
null_model: The observed bias arises from fluctuation, contamination or uncontrolled environmental asymmetry.
control_condition: Use racemic inputs, blanks, contamination controls, sham drives and blinded analysis as applicable.
statistical_method: Preregistered effect estimation and model comparison using replicate distributions.
confounders: Conflation of molecular chirality with macroscopic vortex handedness
replication_rule: Independent laboratories or implementations must reproduce the claimed magnitude and uncertainty under the same declared conditions.
causal_test_method: Manipulate one proposed symmetry-breaking factor while holding reaction and measurement conditions under blinded control.
confusion_guard: Biological homochirality and laboratory asymmetric autocatalysis are established phenomena. A unique cosmic, magnetic, hydrodynamic or 8 Hz origin mechanism is not established.
